refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency metric (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect latency violations.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes close to vehicle to reduce processing delays.
DP3: Centralize teleoperation command processing in a dedicated Teleoperation module.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring and adaptive control to maintain latency within limits.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP3: Centralize teleoperation command processing in a dedicated Teleoperation module.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between Teleoperation module and Vehicle for command execution.
Dependency on Network Link interface performance.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates teleoperation processing, reducing diffusion and enabling focused latency management.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed parameters for latency.
Risk of latency spikes due to network congestion or hardware failures.
Assumes Teleoperation module can be optimized independently without excessive cross-module dependencies.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding explicit DP for latency monitoring (DP4) if latency violations are frequent.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for processing teleoperation commands, managing communication latency, and interfacing with Vehicle and Network Link.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Telemetry data exchanged between Teleoperation and Vehicle for command feedback and status.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Teleoperation receives tasking commands from NGHE Solution or Site Manager.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network interface for communication between Teleoperation and remote operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as Teleoperation module encapsulates latency-sensitive processing; low diffusion due to limited external dependencies.
Vehicle module contains command execution but less suitable for latency management logic.
NGHE Solution is broader and less focused on latency control, leading to higher diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and policies, not latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing teleoperation latency control in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and enables clear ownership and optimization.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact if Teleoperation module interfaces are not well optimized.
Risk of cross-module latency dependencies if Vehicle or Network Link modules introduce delays.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor latency diffusion across Vehicle and Network Link modules; consider interface optimization or partial DP redistribution if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR is measurable and the selected DP logically addresses the latency requirement with clear functional mapping.

diffusion_risks: Latency dependencies on Vehicle and Network Link modules require monitoring.
Potential diffusion if Teleoperation module interfaces are not tightly controlled.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry and network monitoring tools at the Teleoperation module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit latency monitoring DP and IM if latency violations are detected in operation.
Review interface performance with Vehicle and Network Link modules periodically.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a suitable DP, and assigns a clear IM boundary with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect latency violations.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level for detailed sub-FRs.

assumptions: Teleoperation module can be optimized independently.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network or hardware issues.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational conditions.
Interface performance metrics between Teleoperation, Vehicle, and Network Link.
